author: Tertullian

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Other Varying Opinions Among the Valentinians Respecting the Deity, Characteristic Raillery.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

title: Other Varying Opinions Among the Valentinians Respecting the Deity, Characteristic Raillery.

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.text: Others of purer mind, mindful of the honour of the Deity, have, for the purpose of freeing him from the discredit of even single wedlock, preferred assigning no sex whatever to Bythus; and therefore very likely they talk of “this deity” in the neuter gender rather than “this god.”

Others again, on the other hand, speak of him as both masculine and feminine, so that the worthy chronicler Fenestella must not suppose that an hermaphrodite was only to be found among the good people of Luna.
